etymology.description: phonetic 辛 (xīn) + semantic 斤 (jīn, "axe") -> originally "to chop wood"; semantic 木 (mù, "wood") later added -> borrowed for "new"
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicted actions associated with chopping wood, with 斤 (jīn, "axe") indicating the semantic idea and 辛 (xīn, "bitter") providing the sound. Over time, the semantic element 木 (mù, "wood") was incorporated in the bronze inscriptions, closely merging with 辛 (xīn, "bitter"). The character later served as a phonetic loan to express the concept of "new," borrowing its sound from 辛 (xīn), as new growth or change often marks the unfamiliar or novel.

examples.chinese: 我买了一辆新车(我買了一輛新車)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ mǎi le yī liàng xīn chē.

examples.chinese: 他们搬进了新家(他們搬進了新家)。
examples.english: They moved into a new home.

examples.chinese: 新政策实施后(新政策實施後)，公司开始发展得更快(公司開始發展得更快)。
examples.pinyin: Xīn zhèngcè shíshī hòu, gōngsī kāishǐ fāzhǎn de gèng kuài.
examples.english: After the new policy was implemented, the company began to develop faster.
